rescue_reasoning: Climate_screen is explicit_self, so retain despite missing outcome token. The measure is explicitly about disaster response capacity: policy_statement says it will 'use AI and digital tools to improve disaster-site information collection and command' and verbatim_text specifies 'Use AI for information collection to strengthen the security command structure' and 'Consider the utilization of drone videos at disaster sites.' This constitutes emergency management/disaster-response capacity improvement, aligning with Adaptation in the parent_statement context of coping with 'larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.'
policy_statement: TMG will use AI and digital tools to improve disaster-site information collection and command, including enabling web meetings/training and considering drone video use.
mechanism_description: TMG will use AI and digital tools (including web meetings/training and potentially drone video) to improve disaster-site information collection and sharing, strengthening command and coordination during emergencies.
primary_causal_pathway: Climate hazard event → improved situational awareness/coordination → ↓ exposure & vulnerability → ↓ climate-impact damages
causal_mechanism_detail:
1) Change in human activity/system: Emergency management operations shift to AI/digital-enabled data collection, real-time information sharing, and remote coordination/training (e.g., web-based command meetings, drone imagery feeds).  
2) Mediating physical/social process: Improved situational awareness and decision-making speed/quality enhances dispatch, evacuation, resource allocation, and inter-agency coordination during rapidly evolving hazard conditions.  
3) Climate-relevant outcome: Reduced impacts of climate-related disasters (fewer casualties, less property/infrastructure damage, shorter service interruptions), increasing resilience to extreme events.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is reduced loss and disruption from climate-amplified hazards (e.g., storms, floods, heat emergencies) through faster, better-coordinated emergency response enabled by digital/AI tools.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: The mechanism acts on disaster response performance (information → coordination → response effectiveness), which directly reduces hazard impacts—an adaptation pathway.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: Any mitigation effect (e.g., marginal reductions in travel via web meetings) is incidental and not the main climate-relevant outcome; the central intent and effect is resilience during emergencies. Resource efficiency is not the core mechanism, and it does not primarily enhance carbon sinks (so not nature-based).  
3) Edge cases applied: Digitalization/AI can sometimes be mitigation if targeted at optimizing energy/transport emissions; here it is explicitly for disaster-site intelligence and command/coordination, making adaptation dominant.  
4) Confirm applies to ALL instances of this mechanism: Policies described by this mechanism—digital/AI-enabled emergency management capacity improvement—consistently aim to reduce disaster impacts through better response/coordination, fitting adaptation across contexts.  
5) Secondary subtype assignment deferred: Whether a specific policy most affects floods, storms, heatwaves, earthquakes/tsunami, or critical infrastructure resilience depends on policy-level evidence and is not assigned here.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: If a policy framed under this mechanism is primarily used for routine operational efficiency (e.g., reducing fleet fuel use) rather than emergency response, it could shift toward Mitigation/Resource Efficiency; however, the stated mechanism is clearly emergency/disaster management capacity improvement, so Adaptation is appropriate.
key_indicators: "Use AI for information collection"; "strengthen the security command structure"; "put the digital environment in place to collect and share information"; "hold web meetings"; "provide training or guidance"; "utilization of drone videos at disaster sites"
co_benefits: Improved day-to-day emergency/service management efficiency; potential public safety benefits beyond climate hazards; technology innovation capacity
instance_edge_case_notes: Could also be framed as a planning-strategy (modernization/operational improvement) or procurement-requirement (if specified as required tools), but the dominant action is building/implementing digital capability (“put the digital environment in place”), consistent with infrastructure investment.
